records.ety: [OF. deservir, desservir, to merit, L. deservire to serve zealously, be devoted to; de- + servire to serve. See Serve.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Deserved (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Deserving.]

defs: To earn by service; to be worthy of (something due, either good or evil); to merit; to be entitled to; as, the laborer deserves his wages; a work of value deserves praise.
quotes: God exacteth of thee less than thine iniquity deserveth.
John Gay deserved to be a favorite.
Encouragement is not held out to things that deserve reprehension.

defs: To serve; to treat; to benefit.
quotes: A man that hath So well deserved me.

defs: To be worthy of recompense; -- usually with ill or with well.
quotes: One man may merit or deserve of another.
